caption: never feel guilty for eating out too much when I’m supporting a small business ✨  📍 Yamo – 3406 18th St, San Francisco, CA 94110 📍 Taqueria El Farolito – 2779 Mission St, San Francisco, CA 94110 (by 24th St BART) 📍 Panchita's Pupuseria & Restaurant – 3091 16th St, San Francisco, CA 94103 📍 Al Pastor Papi – 232 O’Farrell St, San Francisco, CA 94102 (downtown) 📍 Arizmendi Bakery (Valencia) – 1268 Valencia St, San Francisco, CA 94110 #sfcheapeats #bayareaeats
transcript: I'm on a mission to find the best cheap eats from small businesses in San Francisco. Here are my favorites so far. If you're ever craving some incredible Burmese style noodles, Yamon the Mission is that girl! And one of very few sit-down places where you'll see their entire menu under $10. El Fadolito, off of the 24th Street Parts Station, is truly one of my go-to's for a mission burrito. So generous with their portions. Also love that they close at 3 a.m. Whenever I'm out and about getting drinks in the mission, ending with the Fadolito burrito is a must. For some fresh, spicy salmon poque, Bassa, seafood, express, never disappoints with their quality. For some of the most delicious Papuses you'll find in the Bay Area. And Cheetah's is a spa. I do a great happy hour during the week 3 to 5 p.m. Two Papuses, $8, will you? I did take a gummy before this. Late was calling my name. So this was more around $14. Still very reasonable on price and those plantains were something else. I recently just discovered a new favorite spot for tacos. This is Alpastor Papi downtown. Highly recommend getting their tacos Alpastor, Al Tropo. Basically just the most delicious pork pineapple taco. And they have a great house salsa too. Last but not least, Ari Smelly does a super the week in pizza of the day. I'm so glad that they brought back their pumpkin coconut curry soup. Get that with the slice of their pizza.
address: 3803 32nd St, San Diego, CA 92104, USA

caption: Hawaiian Restaurant in North Georgia! #atlanta #atlantaeats #hawaiian #hawaiianfood #dolewhip #mochi #atl #gainesville
transcript: Did you know about this Hawaiian restaurant north of Atlanta? It was actually crafted by the same family behind King's Hawaiian Roll. The design of this place felt like an island oasis with lush greenery, tropical colors everywhere, and swing chairs along the grassy play area. Some must try things on their menu or their hula whip and local loaf of sliders. Of course they're all made with Hawaiian Rolls. It's super family friendly also. There's a huge, cute friendly outdoor space where they can play while the adults enjoy the refreshing dishes. It's honestly so unique. There's really nothing like this in the area. It's a beautiful slice of Hawaiian culture tucked away in North Georgia. And we weren't expecting the food to be so good. We thought it was just more of a vibe, but the food was actually very flavorful. Don't forget to add this to your summer bucket list. It's called Hello Heel O.
